summary: Russian forces struck a private enterprise in Dnipro, killing 5 and injuring over 20, while Ukraine launched large-scale drone attacks across multiple Russian and occupied regions including Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, Sumy, Poltava, Chernihiv, and Odesa oblasts. A probable fire was reported at the 'Kuban' power transmission facility in Russia, a key energy supply element for Crimea, with mass power outages recorded in occupied Donetsk and parts of Kherson region. The Russian Defense Ministry claimed strikes on Ukrainian fuel-energy and transport infrastructure, destruction of up to 95 Ukrainian personnel in Konstantinovka, and that Russian air defenses shot down 415 Ukrainian drones and 12 guided bombs in one day.
key_points: Russian forces struck a private enterprise in Dnipro, killing 5 people and injuring over 20, with 5 in critical condition (Source: @eRadarrua/98618).

Ukraine launched extensive drone attacks across Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, Sumy, Poltava, Chernihiv, and Odesa oblasts, including KAB strikes on Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/66764).

A probable fire at the 'Kuban' power transmission facility in Russia disrupted energy supply to Crimea, with mass power outages in occupied Donetsk and parts of Kherson region (Source: @UkraineNow/82248).

Russian MoD claimed air defenses shot down 415 Ukrainian drones and 12 guided bombs in one day, and that Russian forces killed up to 95 Ukrainian soldiers in Konstantinovka (Source: @tass_agency/382892).
The commander of Ukraine's 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade, Vladimir Kononnikov, was found dead; circumstances under investigation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31231).
Russia deployed approximately 20 groups of Shahed-136-type drones launched from the Kursk region (Source: @eRadarrua/98563).
Ukraine faces power outages due to abnormal heat, with every 3°C increase in Kyiv adding approximately 100 MW of load on the grid (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31234).
Russian forces struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in the Nikolaev region with Geranium drones, hitting boat and unmanned craft depots (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces struck a private enterprise in Dnipro, killing 5 and injuring over 20, while Ukraine launched coordinated drone strikes across at least eight oblasts including KAB munitions on Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk regions. A probable fire at the 'Kuban' power transmission facility in Russia disrupted electricity supply to Crimea, with blackouts reported in occupied Donetsk and Kherson regions. The Russian Defense Ministry claimed significant daily attrition of Ukrainian forces and infrastructure, including 415 drones and 12 guided bombs shot down in a single day.

points: Ukrainian drone operators conducted extensive strikes across multiple oblasts: 3 drones on Zaporizhzhia, KAB strikes on Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk regions, drones on Pavlohrad, Kryvyi Rih, Kharkiv, Sumy, Poltava (Kotelva and Zinkiv), Chernihiv, and Odesa regions (Source: @eRadarrua/98596, @kpszsu/66764).
Drone operators from the Rubicon unit destroyed Ukrainian Armed Forces firing positions from the air, according to source material (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31235).
The commander of the 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade, Vladimir Kononnikov, was found dead, reported by the 'South' operational command; reasons being established (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31231).

Ukraine faces widespread power outages due to abnormal heat; every 3°C increase in Kyiv adds approximately 100 MW of load on the grid, with some energy infrastructure still under repair from previous strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31234).

Ukraine will require nearly $146 billion in external financing in 2026–2029, stated Finance Minister Sergey Marchenko following meetings with UK and Lithuanian counterparts in Gdansk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224).

NABU detained Servant of the People MP Sergey Kuzminykh on suspicion of receiving kickbacks for helping companies win medical equipment supply tenders in Zhytomyr region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31236).

A probable fire broke out at the 'Kuban' power transmission facility in Russia, described as a key energy supply element for Crimea, with power lines running underground and under the Kerch Strait (Source: @UkraineNow/82248).
Mass power outages were recorded in occupied Donetsk and temporarily occupied parts of Kherson region following the 'Kuban' facility incident (Source: @UkraineNow/82248).
Russian MoD claimed strikes on Ukrainian fuel-energy and transport infrastructure, and destruction of Ukrainian long-range drone storage and launch sites over one day (Source: @tass_agency/382892).
Russian air defenses shot down 12 guided bombs and 415 Ukrainian fixed-wing drones in one day, according to the Russian Defense Ministry (Source: @tass_agency/382892).
Russian forces used Geranium drones to strike a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in the Nikolaev region, targeting boat and unmanned craft depots and personnel (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222).
Russian artillery destroyed a Ukrainian military shelter in Konstantinovka, according to source material (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31242).
Russia launched approximately 20 groups of Shahed-136-type drones from the Kursk region (Source: @eRadarrua/98563).
Russian forces in Konstantinovka destroyed up to 95 Ukrainian military personnel in one day according to Russian MoD claims (Source: @tass_agency/382892).

Russian forces in Krasny Liman, advancing in a western direction, captured 48 buildings and destroyed more than 40 Ukrainian military personnel and a US-made HMMWV armored vehicle (Source: @tass_agency/382892).
Russian artillery destroyed a Ukrainian military shelter in Konstantinovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31242).

Ukrainian drones struck Kharkiv from the north, with additional drones targeting Zmiiv (reactive type), Chuhuyiv (reactive type), and Staryi Merchik/Lyubotyn (two strike drones) (Source: @eRadarrua/98571).
Drones were reported targeting Bohodukhov from the north and Kov'yagi from the south in Kharkiv oblast (Source: @kpszsu/66761).
Ukrainian forces conducted mortar strikes on a multi-story residential building in Kakhovka, Kherson region, wounding an elderly man (Source: @tass_agency/382895).

Ukrainian drones targeted Sumy oblast from multiple directions: toward Hlushiv from the east, toward Trostianets from the north, and toward Yampil from the northeast, with 3 drones in the Trostianets-Chupakhivka area including 2 reactive types (Source: @kpszsu/66737, @eRadarrua/98602, @kpszsu/66772).
Drones targeted Kharkiv from the north and Sumi from the northeast (Source: @kpszsu/66757).

Chernihiv oblast saw drone activity toward Paryfiyivka/Ichnya ('Molniya' type), Novhorod-Siverskyi, Prysluk/Prut, and Horodnia from the north (Source: @eRadarrua/98569, @eRadarrua/98582, @eRadarrua/98568, @kpszsu/66764).
Ukrainian authorities warned of a threat of ballistic weapon use from the southeast (Source: @kpszsu/66754).

Zaporizhzhia faced multiple drone attacks: 3 drones from the south, a reactive drone in the Tokmka district heading toward Nikopol, and KAB strikes on Mala Katerynivka (Source: @eRadarrua/98585, @eRadarrua/98582, @eRadarrua/98596).

Dnipropetrovsk oblast saw extensive drone activity: KAB strikes, drones toward Aviatorske/Dnipro, Zhelenodolsk from the southwest, Piatykhatky, Nikopol from the east, and Pavlohrad from the southwest (Source: @kpszsu/66750, @kpszsu/66715, @kpszsu/66718, @kpszsu/66766, @kpszsu/66742).
Odesa region: a reactive drone was reported toward Vylkova from the Black Sea waters, and another toward Artsyz from the southeast (Source: @kpszsu/66743, @kpszsu/66756).

In Sevastopol, Russian authorities reported repelling 2 Ukrainian attacks, shooting down 29 drones in one day, with 4 private houses damaged and forest fires covering 8 hectares from falling drone debris (Source: @tass_agency/382868).

Ukraine deployed a large-scale combined drone assault using multiple types including KAB (guided aerial bombs), reactive drones, Shahed-136-type, Molniya, and Merlin-type drones across at least eight oblasts simultaneously (Source: @eRadarrua/98596, @kpszsu/66764, @eRadarrua/98563).
Russia launched approximately 20 groups of Shahed-136-type attack drones from the Kursk region (Source: @eRadarrua/98563).
Russian MoD claimed air defenses intercepted 415 Ukrainian fixed-wing drones and 12 guided bombs in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/382892).
Russian forces are actively deploying mobile fire groups armed with FPV-interceptor drones, according to Defense Minister Belousov (Source: @tass_agency/382871).

In Sevastopol, 29 Ukrainian drones were shot down in one day, with falling debris igniting forest fires across 8 hectares and 13 fire foci of elevated complexity detected (Source: @tass_agency/382868).

key_developments: Russian forces struck a private enterprise in Dnipro, killing 5 people and injuring over 20, with 5 in critical condition; medical assistance being provided (Source: @eRadarrua/98618).

A probable fire at the 'Kuban' power transmission facility in Russia disrupted energy supply to Crimea, with mass power outages recorded in occupied Donetsk and parts of Kherson region (Source: @UkraineNow/82248).

Ukraine launched coordinated drone strikes across Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, Sumy, Poltava, Chernihiv, and Odesa oblasts, including KAB munitions on Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk regions (Source: @eRadarrua/98596, @kpszsu/66764).
Russian MoD claimed daily destruction of 415 Ukrainian drones and 12 guided bombs by air defenses, plus up to 95 Ukrainian personnel killed in Konstantinovka (Source: @tass_agency/382892).
The commander of Ukraine's 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade, Vladimir Kononnikov, was found dead; the 'South' operational command is investigating (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31231).
Russia deployed approximately 20 groups of Shahed-136-type drones launched from the Kursk region (Source: @eRadarrua/98563).
Ukraine faces power grid strain from abnormal heat adding 100 MW of load per 3°C increase in Kyiv, compounding damage from prior infrastructure strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31234).
sections.threat_assessment: The conflict is intensifying with both sides conducting large-scale strikes on infrastructure and military targets. Russia's deployment of approximately 20 Shahed-136 drone groups from Kursk and strikes on Ukrainian training camps indicate continued offensive operations. Ukraine's multi-oblast drone campaign demonstrates sustained long-range strike capability targeting Russian logistics and energy infrastructure. The 'Kuban' facility fire, if confirmed, would represent a significant blow to Crimea's energy supply. The death of a Ukrainian brigade commander and Russian claims of heavy daily Ukrainian casualties suggest sustained attrition on the eastern front around Konstantinovka and Krasny Liman.
sections.regional_response: The United States and Iran reportedly reached an agreement to halt mutual strikes and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, with further negotiations expected in Qatar. President Biden accused Trump of undermining NATO and choosing Putin over American allies at the Democratic Party convention in Maryland. Estonian Foreign Minister Tsakhkna stated some EU countries no longer wish to pressure Russia, anticipating a negotiated settlement. Russian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Zakharova accused Ukraine of conducting 11 drone incidents against EU territory in the past three months. Peru's prosecutor opened a human trafficking investigation after dozens of citizens were allegedly lured to Russia with false promises of lucrative work and sent to the front in Ukraine.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukraine's Rubicon drone unit operators are confirmed active in destroying Ukrainian Armed Forces firing positions from the air. Russian mobile fire groups equipped with FPV-interceptor drones are being actively deployed according to Defense Minister Belousov. The Russian MoD briefing claimed Ukrainian forces lost over 1,500 military personnel across all sectors of the front in one day, though this figure cannot be independently verified. Ukraine's financing needs for 2026–2029 are estimated at nearly $146 billion. Russian forces claimed to have captured 48 buildings in Krasny Liman advancing westward. The heatwave affecting Ukraine is compounding strain on energy infrastructure already degraded by repeated strikes.
